intro.headline: Where to find what no travel guide tells you.
intro.blurb: Pads, tampons, cups, period pain meds, intimate hygiene — by city, by chemist, by neighbourhood. Sourced from contributors who've actually been on their period in these places.

general.title: Pads (Whisper, Stayfree, Sofy)
general.summary: Available at every chemist and most kirana stores nationwide. ₹50–₹150 per pack. Ultra-thin, overnight, and panty-liner variants stocked everywhere except remote villages.

general.title: Tampons (OB, Tampax, Stayfree)
general.summary: Metro cities only — Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, Chennai, Pune, Hyderabad, Goa. Apollo Pharmacy, Health & Glow, and Nature's Basket are the most reliable. Outside metros, bring a 1-month supply.

general.title: Menstrual cups & discs
general.summary: SheCup, Sirona, Pee Safe Imbue and Boondh are the easiest to find. Stocked at Apollo Pharmacy, Wellness Forever, and online via Amazon / Nykaa with same-day delivery in metros. Cup users — boil before and after each cycle.

general.title: Pee Safe & intimate hygiene
general.summary: Pee Safe (toilet seat sanitiser spray) is sold at every chemist and most petrol-pump convenience stores. Sirona intimate wash, V-Wash, and Clean & Dry stocked at any Health & Glow or Apollo.

general.title: Period pain (Meftal Spas, Combiflam)
general.summary: Over the counter at any chemist. Carry a prescription for stronger NSAIDs if you have severe cramps. Heat patches (Sofy Antibac, EvaTouch) widely available in metro cities.

byCity.cups: Apollo Pharmacy only — ask at counter, often kept behind
byCity.notes: Yoga ashrams generally stock pads at reception in emergencies. Tampons are scarce — bring your own if you'll be here more than a week.

byCity.cups: Wellness Forever (Mapusa, Vagator) — full SheCup + Sirona range
byCity.notes: Tourist areas fully stocked. Ask for the women's section — sometimes shelved away from the main aisle.

byCity.pads: Reliance Smart, Big Bazaar, every chemist
byCity.tampons: Apollo Pharmacy, MedPlus (C-Scheme branch most reliable)
byCity.cups: Apollo Pharmacy C-Scheme; online order via Amazon for same-day
byCity.notes: Old City chemists are hit-or-miss for tampons. Stay near C-Scheme or Vaishali Nagar if supply matters.

byCity.tampons: Not available — go to Hospet (13km) for Apollo Pharmacy
byCity.cups: Not available locally — order online before you arrive
byCity.notes: Remote site. Pack a full cycle's worth before coming. Hospet town has one Apollo with limited stock.

byCity.tampons: Apollo Pharmacy, Lanka — usually in stock
byCity.cups: Online only (Amazon, Nykaa) — no local stockists
byCity.notes: Older parts of the city have fewer options. Lanka and Sigra are your reliable zones.

byCity.tampons: Apollo, MedPlus, Wellness Forever — fully stocked
byCity.cups: Wellness Forever Indiranagar; SheCup HQ ships same-day
byCity.notes: Best-stocked city in India for women's products. Cubbon Park area also reliable.

byCity.tampons: Mall Road Apollo Pharmacy — not always in stock
byCity.cups: Not available locally — order online before arrival
byCity.notes: Off-season (Nov–Feb) supply gets thin. Bring extra. Higher altitude = heavier flow for some, plan accordingly.

byCity.pads: Every chemist, also stocked at Lulu Mall
byCity.tampons: Apollo Pharmacy MG Road; Lulu Mall Hypermarket
byCity.notes: Kerala chemists are well-stocked. Backwater stays — pack supplies, no shops nearby once you're on a houseboat.

packing: 1 month supply of your preferred period product (especially tampons / cups outside metros).
A small zip pouch with 2–3 emergency pads in your day bag — chemists close early in many small towns.
Heating pad or self-heating patches if you have cramps. Indian chemists carry generic versions.
Period underwear (Saathi, Sirona, Bella) is Indian-made and great for trains / long bus rides — bring 2 pairs.
Reusable cloth pouch for used products when you're hiking or in remote stays without bins.

communityLink.label: Read: Managing your period while travelling solo in India
communityLink.blurb: 21 replies, 69 helpful marks — contributor stories on cup management in low-bathroom places, pain meds that work, and what to do when you're caught out.
